1.source: 第三回 託內兄如海薦西賓　接外孫賈母惜孤女
1.translation: Chapter : Ruhai Recommends a Tutor to His Brother In Law; Grandmother Jia Sends for Her Motherless Granddaughter

2.source: 卻說雨村忙回頭看時，不是別人，乃是當日同僚一案參革的張如圭。
2.translation: It so happened that, when Yucun turned around quickly to look, he saw none other than Zhang Rugui, a former colleague who had also been dismissed from office because of the same case.

3.translation: He was a native of this land and was living at home after his dismissal.

4.source: 今打聽得都中奏準起復舊員之信，他便四下裡尋情找門路，忽遇見雨村，故忙道喜。
4.translation: Zhang had heard that the capital was reinstating former officials, and he had been busy pulling strings to figure out a way back into office when he suddenly ran across Yucun.   And so Zhang had eagerly come to congratulate him.

5.source: 二人見了禮，張如圭便將此信告知雨村。
5.translation: After a brief greeting, Zhang Rugui told Yucun the good news.

6.source: 雨村歡喜。忙忙敘了兩句，遂作別各自回家。
6.translation: Yucun was delighted.   They chatted briefly, after which they parted.

7.source: 冷子興聽得此言，便忙獻計，令雨村央求林如海，轉向都中去央煩賈政。
7.translation: When Leng Zixing heard about this, he quickly proposed a plan.   He suggested that Yucun should ask Lin Ruhai to request a favor for him from Jia Zheng in the capital.

8.source: 雨村領其意而別，回至館中，忙尋邸報看真確了。
8.translation: Yucun understood and left, returning to his room at the Lin residence.   He hurriedly looked for a copy of the court gazette to verify the news.

9.source: 次日，面謀林如海。如海道：「天緣湊巧：因賤荊去世，都中家岳母念及小女無人依傍，前已遣了男女船隻來接，因小女未曾大痊，故未及行，此刻正思送女進京。
9.translation:
The next day, he went to talk about it personally with Lin Ruhai.

 “What a coincidence,” said Lin Ruhai.   “Since my wife passed away, my mother-in-law in the capital has sent servants and boats to fetch my daughter, out of concern that she has nobody to rely on.

10.source: 因向蒙教誨之恩，未經酬報，遇此機會，豈有不盡心圖報之理！
10.translation: “Because I’ve been in your debt for your instructional kindness, and because I haven’t yet paid you, how could I neglect this opportunity to express my gratitude to you?

11.source: 弟已預籌之，修下薦書一封，託內兄務為周全，方可稍盡弟之鄙誠。
11.translation: I’ve already made a plan, and have written a letter of recommendation to my brother-in-law [Jia Zheng], asking him to arrange everything properly so that I can fulfill my humble obligations.

12.source: 即有所費，弟於內家信中寫明，不勞吾兄多慮。
12.translation: I’ll mention any expenses you incur in my letter, so that you won’t have to worry about such things.”

13.source: 」雨村一面打恭，謝不釋口，一面又問：「不知令親大人現居何職？
13.translation: Yucun bowed deeply and poured forth an endless stream of thanks.   “Might I ask,” he added, “what official position your esteemed relative currently holds?

14.source: 只怕晚生草率，不敢進謁。
14.translation: I fear being presumptuous — I don’t want to call upon him without proper regard.”

15.source: 」如海笑道：「若論舍親，與尊兄猶系一家，乃榮公之孫。
15.translation: “As for my humble relatives,” smiled Lin Ruhai, “they share ancestral ties with you, bing grandsons of the Duke of Rong.

16.source: 大內兄現襲一等將軍之職，名赦，字恩侯。
16.translation: My elder brother-in-law has inherited the rank of First-Class General.   His given name is She, and his courtesy name is Enhou.

17.source: 二內兄名政，字存周，現任工部員外郎。
17.translation: “My younger brother-in-law’s given name is Zheng, his courtesy name is Cunzhou, and he serves as Undersecretary in the Ministry of Works.

18.source: 其為人謙恭厚道，大有祖父遺風，非膏粱輕薄之流，故弟致書煩託。
18.translation: He conducts himself with humility and integrity, embodying his grandfather’s noble character — nothing like those frivolous sons of privilege.   This is why I decided to trouble him with a recommendation letter.

19.source: 否則不但有汙尊兄清操，即弟亦不屑為矣。
19.translation: Otherwise, not only would it stain your unsullied virtue, but even I’d figure it a lost cause.”

20.source: 」雨村聽了，心下方信了昨日子興之言，於是又謝了林如海。
20.translation: When Yucun heard this, he realized that Leng Zixing’s words from the day before were true.   He thanked Lin Ruhai again.

21.source: 如海又說：「擇了出月初二日小女入都，吾兄即同路而往，豈不兩便？
21.translation: “We’ve selected the second day of the next month for my daughter to leave for the capital,” continued Ruhai.   “Wouldn’t it be suitable for you to travel together with her?” Yucun agreed deferentially, though he felt extremely satisfied.

22.source: 」雨村唯唯聽命，心中十分得意。
22.translation: “Wouldn’t it be suitable for you to travel together with her?” Yucun agreed deferentially, though he felt extremely satisfied.   Ruhai then prepared gifts and the farewell arrangements, all of which Yucun accepted politely.

23.source: 如海遂打點禮物並餞行之事，雨村一一領了。
23.translation: Ruhai then prepared gifts and the farewell arrangements, all of which Yucun accepted politely.

24.source: 那女學生原不忍離親而去，無奈他外祖母必欲其往，且兼如海說：「汝父年已半百，再無續室之意；且汝多病，年又極小，上無親母教養，下無姊妹扶持：今去依傍外祖母及舅氏姊妹，正好減我內顧之憂，如何不去？
24.translation: The schoolgirl couldn’t bear to leave her father, but she had no choice, since her grandmother insisted that she must go.   Additionally, Ruhai reasoned with her, saying “your father is already  and has no intentions on remarrying.   You’re often sick, you’re still so young, and you’ve got no mother to guide you from above, nor any sisters to support you from below.

25.source: 」黛玉聽了，方灑淚拜別，隨了奶孃及榮府中幾個老婦，登舟而去。
25.translation: After hearing this, Daiyu could only wipe away her tears, bow in farewell, and then board the boat.   Together with her nurse and several elderly maids from the Rong Mansion, they departed at last.

26.source: 雨村另有一隻船，帶了兩個小童，依附黛玉而行。
26.translation: Yucun, meanwhile, followed behind in a separate vessel with two young male servants, trailing closely behind Daiyu.

28.source: 一日，到了京都，雨村先整了衣冠，帶著童僕，拿了「宗侄」的名帖，至榮府門上投了。
28.translation: On the day that they arrived in the capital, Yucun first straightened his attire and hat, and then took a name card inscribed with “Clan Nephew” and presented it at the gates of the Rong mansion, accompanied by his servant.

29.source: 彼時賈政已看了妹丈之書，即忙請入相會。
29.translation: By that time Jia Zheng had already read his brother-in-law’s letter, and promptly invited Yucun in to meet him.

30.source: 見雨村相貌魁偉，言談不俗，且這賈政最喜的是讀書人，禮賢下士，拯溺救危，大有祖風，況又系妹丈致意，因此優待雨村，更又不同，便極力幫助。
30.translation: Seeing that Yucun was fairly large and spoke with uncommon refinement, and since Jia Zheng had always liked scholars — honoring worthies and aiding those in distress, in his ancestral tradition — he received Yucun with exceptional courtesy, in particular because of his brother-in-law’s recommendation.

31.source: 題奏之日，謀了一個復職。
31.translation: By the time the proposal was officially processed, Jia Yucun had already managed to secure his reinstatement.

32.source: 不上兩月，便選了金陵應天府，辭了賈政，擇日到任去了。
32.translation: In less than two months he was assigned to the Jinling Yingtian Prefect.   After saying his goodbyes to Jia Zheng, he chose an auspicious day to take up his office.

33.source:
不在話下。

且說黛玉自那日棄舟登岸時，便有榮府打發轎子並拉行李的車輛伺候。
33.translation:
We’ll set that story aside for now.

 When Lin Daiyu arrived on the boat, there were sedan chairs and carts for her luggage waiting for her, all of which had been sent by the Rong mansion.

34.source: 這黛玉嘗聽得母親說他外祖母家與別人家不同，他近日所見的這幾個三等的僕婦，吃穿用度，已是不凡，何況今至其家。
34.translation: Now, Daiyu had often heard her mother talk about how different her maternal grandmother’s family was from all the others.   And yet even the lower ranking maids and servants she had been with over the last few days were unusually lavish in the way they dressed and the food they ate, and were refined in how they handled themselves.

35.source: 因此步步留心，時時在意，不多說一句話，不多行一步路，恐被人恥笑了去。
35.translation: And so she remained vigilant in every step she took, measuring each word and weighing each action, terrified that a single mistake would invite ridicule.

36.source: 自上了轎，進了城，從紗窗向外瞧了一瞧，其街市之繁華，人煙之阜盛，自非別處可比。
36.translation: Daiyu peered out the gauze window from the moment she stepped into the sedan chair and was taken into the city.   She looked at the bustling streets, the teeming crowds, and a spectacle of prosperity unmatched anywhere else.

37.source: 又行了半日，忽見街北蹲著兩個大石獅子，三間獸頭大門，門前列坐著十來個華冠麗服之人。
37.translation: After traveling for a while, she saw two massive stone lions crouching on the northern side of the street.   These lions framed a gateway of three arches that was crowned with the heads of snarling beasts.   In front of the gate sat around a dozen men in fancy dress and with ornate headdresses.

38.source: 正門不開，只東西兩角門有人出入。
38.translation: The main gate did not open; only the two side gates — one to the east, one to the west — would open for entering and exiting.

39.source: 正門之上有一匾，匾上大書「敕造寧國府」五個大字。
39.translation: Above the main gate hung a large plaque inscribed with the words “Ningguo Mansion, Commissioned By Imperial Decree.”

40.source: 黛玉想道：「這是外祖的長房了。
40.translation: “This must be the eldest branch of my grandfather’s family,” thought Daiyu to herself.

41.source: 」又往西不遠，照樣也是三間大門，方是榮國府，卻不進正門，只由西角門而入。
41.translation: A little bit further west stood another grand triple-arched gate that was identical in design.   This was Rongguo mansion.   Yet, instead of entering through the main gate, they turned towards the western side entrance.

42.source: 轎子抬著走了一射之地，將轉彎時，便歇了轎，後面的婆子也都下來了。
42.translation: The men carrying the sedan chair carried her around another  meters before halting at a turn.   At that point, they set the chair down and the older female servants following behind all dismounted.

43.source: 另換了四個眉目秀潔十七八歲的小廝上來抬著轎子，眾婆子步下跟隨。
43.translation: Four clean-cut servant boys, around  or  years old and with delicate features, replaced the bearers and lifted up the sedan chair.   The women followed on foot.

44.source: 至一垂花門前落下，眾小廝俱肅然退出，眾婆子上前打起轎簾，扶黛玉下了轎。
44.translation: They stopped before the drooping flower gate, which led to the women’s quarters.   The young men then left in silence.   The female servants stepped forward, raised the curtain, and assisted Daiyu as she exited the sedan chair.

45.source: 黛玉扶著婆子的手，進了垂花門。
45.translation: Daiyu grabbed to the hand of one of the older maids and stepped through the drooping flower gate.

46.source: 兩邊是超手遊廊，正中是穿堂，當地放著一個紫檀架子大理石屏風。
46.translation: There were winding corridors on both sides, while a central passage hall stood in front of her.   In the center of that hall was a large screen, carved in purple sandalwood and marble.

47.source: 轉過屏風，小小三間廳房，廳後便是正房大院。
47.translation: As she passed around the large screen, Daiyu entered a small three room reception hall.

48.source: 正面五間上房，皆是雕樑畫棟。
48.translation: Beyond that was the central courtyard, which was flanked by a main residence with five rooms and was adorned with carved beams and painted pillars.

49.source: 兩邊穿山遊廊廂房，掛著各色鸚鵡畫眉等雀鳥。
49.translation: There were wings on both sides, adorned with artificial rockeries, that hummed with the chatter of caged parrots, thrushes, and other songbirds.

50.source: 臺階上坐著幾個穿紅著綠的丫頭，一見他們來了，都笑迎上來，道：「剛才老太太還念著呢，可巧就來了。
50.translation: A group of maids wearing red and green robes sat chatting on the steps to the terrace.   As soon as they spotted the arriving group, they rose with smiling faces, saying “Grandmother Jia was just talking about you!

51.translation: How lucky that you’ve arrived now!” Three or four of them then ran over to lift the curtain to the doorway, while shouts of “Miss Lin has arrived!” echoed out from within.

52.source: 一面聽得人說：「林姑娘來了！
52.translation: As soon as they spotted the arriving group, they rose with smiling faces, saying “Grandmother Jia was just talking about you!   How lucky that you’ve arrived now!” Three or four of them then ran over to lift the curtain to the doorway, while shouts of “Miss Lin has arrived!” echoed out from within.

55.source: 黛玉方進房，只見兩個人扶著一位鬢髮如銀的老母迎上來。
55.translation: As Daiyu entered the room, she saw two attendants supporting an elderly lady with silver-white hair coming forward to greet her.

56.source: 黛玉知是外祖母，正欲下拜，早被外祖母抱住，摟入懷中，「心肝兒肉」叫著大哭起來。
56.translation: Daiyu knew that this was her grandmother.   Just as she was about to kneel in greeting, the old lady hugged her, clutching her tightly to her bosom.

57.source: 當下侍立之人無不落淚，黛玉也哭個不休。
57.translation: By now all the attendants standing nearby were in tears, and Daiyu cried without ceasing.

58.source: 待眾人慢慢勸解住了，那黛玉方拜見了外祖母，賈母方一一指與黛玉道：「這是你大舅母。
58.translation: Daiyu was only able to finally pay her respects to her grandmother after the others had gradually comforted her.   Grandmother Jia then introduced each family member in turn: “This is your elder uncle’s wife.

59.source: 這是二舅母。這是你先前珠大哥的媳婦珠大嫂子。
59.translation: This is your second uncle’s wife.   And this is your late cousin Zhu’s wife, your sister-in-law Zhu.”

60.source: 」黛玉一一拜見了。賈母又叫：「請姑娘們來。
60.translation: Daiyu paid her respects to each of them in turn.   And then Grandmother Jia spoke again: “Fetch the young ladies.

61.source: 今日遠客來了，可以不必上學去。
61.translation: We have a guest from a long way away today, so they don’t need to attend school.” After everybody agreed, two of the maids departed to accomplish this.

62.source: 」眾人答應了一聲，便去了兩個。
62.translation: Daiyu paid her respects to each of them in turn.   And then Grandmother Jia spoke again: “Fetch the young ladies.   We have a guest from a long way away today, so they don’t need to attend school.” After everybody agreed, two of the maids departed to accomplish this.

63.source: 不一時，只見三個奶媽並五六個丫鬟擁著三位姑娘來了：第一個，肌膚微豐，身材閤中，腮凝新荔，鼻膩鵝脂，溫柔沉默，觀之可親；第二個，削肩細腰，長挑身材，鴨蛋臉兒，俊眼修眉，顧盼神飛，文彩精華，見之忘俗；第三個，身量未足，形容尚小。
63.translation:
Before long, three nurses and five or six maids escorted three young ladies into the room.

 The first was slightly plump, of medium height, with cheeks as smooth and as fresh as lychee flesh and a nose as delicate as curdled cream.

64.source: 其釵環裙襖，三人皆是一樣的妝束。
64.translation:
The third had a childish frame and was not yet fully formed.

 The three sisters were dressed identically, wearing the same hairpins, bracelets, skirts, and jackets.

65.source: 黛玉忙起身迎上來見禮，互相廝認。
65.translation: Daiyu quickly rose to greet them, and they exchanged formal bows.

66.source: 歸了坐位，丫鬟送上茶來。
66.translation: After they took their seats again, the maids served tea.

67.source: 不過敘些黛玉之母如何得病，如何請醫服藥，如何送死發喪。
67.translation: Their conversation turned to how Daiyu’s mother had fallen ill, how the they had requested treatment from the doctors, how she passed away, and how her funeral had gone.

68.source: 不免賈母又傷感起來，因說：「我這些女孩兒，所疼的獨有你母親，今一旦先我而亡，不得見面，怎不傷心！
68.translation: This caused Grandmother Jia to be overcome with grief again.   “Of all my daughters,” she said, “your mother was the one I cherished most.   Now she has left this world before me, and I can’t see her again.

69.source: 」說著，攜了黛玉的手，又哭起來。
69.translation: How can my heart not break?” Saying this, she took Daiyu’s hand and began weeping again.

70.source: 眾人都忙相勸慰，方略略止住。
70.translation: It was only after everybody ran over to comfort her that she gradually composed herself.

71.source: 眾人見黛玉年紀雖小，其舉止言談不俗，身體面貌雖弱不勝衣，卻有一段風流態度，便知他有不足之症。
71.translation: Though she was young, Daiyu’s refined speech and graceful demeanor set her apart.   Her delicate frame was almost too small for her clothes, and yet she had a romantic grace about her.   The onlookers could tell at once that she suffered from some kind of sickness.

72.source: 因問：「常服何藥？為何不治好了？
72.translation: “What medicine do you normally take,” they asked her, “and why hasn’t it cured you?”

73.source: 」黛玉道：「我自來如此，從會吃飯時便吃藥到如今了。
73.translation: “I’ve been this way since I was born, replied Daiyu.   “I’ve been taking medicine ever since I started eating.

74.source: 經過多少名醫，總未見效。
74.translation: Many famous doctors have treated me, but none have succeeded.

75.source: 那一年，我才三歲，聽說來了一個癩頭和尚，說要化我去出家，我父母自是不從。
75.translation: When I was just three years old, a scabby-headed monk came and said he wanted to take me away to become a nun.   My parents, of course, refused.

76.source: 他又說：『既捨不得他，但只怕他的病一生也不能好的！
76.translation: “‘Since you cannot bear to part with her,’ the monk said, ‘know this: her illness will never be cured in this lifetime.

77.source: 若要好時，除非從此以後總不許見哭聲，除父母之外，凡有外親一概不見，方可平安了此一生。
77.translation: From now on, she cannot hear any weeping — and she must never meet relatives other than her parents.

78.source: 』這和尚瘋瘋癲癲說了這些不經之談，也沒人理他。
78.translation: “That crazy monk then rambled on with all sorts of absurd mumblings, and nobody paid any attention to them.

79.source: 如今還是吃人蔘養榮丸。
79.translation: And I still eat ginseng pills to this day.”

80.source: 」賈母道：「這正好，我這裡正配丸藥呢，叫他們多配一料就是了。
80.translation: “How fortunate,” said Grandmother Jia.   “We’re currently preparing medical pills here.   I’ll just have them make an additional batch for you.”

83.source: 一語未完，只聽後院中有笑語聲，說：「我來遲了，沒得迎接遠客！
83.translation: Suddenly they could hear laughter from the courtyard behind them.   “I’m late!” a voice cried.   “I’ve missed the chance to welcome our guest!”

84.source: 」黛玉思忖道：「這些人個個皆斂聲屏氣如此，這來者是誰，這樣放誕無禮？
84.translation: “Who could this be,” thought Daiyu to herself, “speaking so rudely while everybody else is solemn and silent?”

85.source: 」心下想時，只見一群媳婦丫鬟擁著一個麗人從後房門進來。
85.translation: As she was pondering this, she saw a group of maids and female servants surrounding a beautiful woman, who entered in through the back door.

86.source: 這個人打扮與姑娘們不同：彩繡輝煌，恍若神妃仙子。
86.translation: This woman was dressed differently than the other girls.   She was resplendent in embroidered silks, and shone with the radiance of a celestial beauty.

87.source: 頭上戴著金絲八寶攢珠髻，綰著朝陽五鳳掛珠釵；項上戴著赤金盤螭纓絡圈；身上穿著縷金百蝶穿花大紅雲緞窄裉襖，外罩五彩刻絲石青銀鼠褂；下著翡翠撒花洋縐裙。
87.translation: Her hair featured an ornate gold and jeweled headpiece, pinned with a Morning Sun hairpin featuring five phoenixes with pearls suspended from their beaks.   Around her neck hung a gleaming red and gold necklace with coiled dragon designs.   She wore a fitted crimson robe embroidered with golden butterflies and flowers, on top of which was a blue-green brocade jacket lined with white fur.

88.source: 一雙丹鳳三角眼，兩彎柳葉吊梢眉。
88.translation: She had a pair of phoenix eyes, sharp and upturned, and two willow-leaf eyebrow that were arched upwards.

89.translation: She was slender, and there was a radiant sensuality about her.

90.source: 粉面含春威不露，丹脣未啟笑先聞。
90.translation: Her scarlet lips had not yet parted, and yet her laughter rang out first.

91.source: 黛玉連忙起身接見。賈母笑道：「你不認得他。
91.translation: Lin Daiyu quickly rose to greet her.   “You don’t know her,” laughed Grandmother Jia.

92.source: 他是我們這裡有名的一個『潑辣貨』，南京所謂『辣子』你只叫他鳳辣子就是了。
92.translation: It’s what we call a ‘hot pepper’ here in Nanjing.

93.source: 」黛玉正不知以何稱呼，眾姊妹都忙告訴黛玉道：「這是璉二嫂子。
93.translation: Daiyu was just wondering how to address her when the sisters hurriedly explained, “This is Sister Lian, our second brother Lian’s wife.”

94.source: 」黛玉雖不曾識面，聽見他母親說過：「大舅賈赦之子賈璉娶的就是二舅母王氏的內侄女，自幼假充男兒教養，學名叫做王熙鳳。
94.translation: Though Daiyu had never met her before, she remembered that her mother had mentioned her.   “Jia Lian,” her mother had told her, “the son of your older uncle Jia She, married the niece of your second uncle’s wife Lady Wang.

95.translation: Daiyu smiled politely and curtseyed, addressing her as “sister-in-law.”

97.source: 這熙鳳攜著黛玉的手，上下細細打量一回，便仍送至賈母身邊坐下，因笑道：「天下真有這樣標緻人兒！
97.translation: She then took Daiyu back to sit beside Grandmother Jia.   “So this is what a true beauty looks like!” said Xifeng with a smile.

98.source: 我今日才算看見了！況且這通身的氣派竟不像老祖宗的外孫女兒，竟是個嫡親的孫女兒似的。
98.translation: “I finally saw perfection today!   And such noble grace!   She seems more like a full blooded granddaughter of Grandmother Jia than a grandchild from her daughter’s side.

99.source: 怨不得老祖宗天天嘴裡心裡放不下。
99.translation: No wonder Grandmother Jia speaks of you day and night, holding you close to her heart!   But I feel so sorry for you, poor cousin.

100.source: 只可憐我這妹妹這麼命苦：怎麼姑媽偏就去世了呢！
100.translation: But I feel so sorry for you, poor cousin.   What cruel fate to lose your dear mother so suddenly!” As she spoke, she dabbed at her eyes with a handkerchief.

101.translation: What cruel fate to lose your dear mother so suddenly!” As she spoke, she dabbed at her eyes with a handkerchief.

102.source: 賈母笑道：「我才好了，你又來招我。
102.translation: “I was just feeling better,” said Grandmother Jia with a smile.

103.source: 你妹妹遠路才來，身子又弱，也才勸住了。
103.translation: Your cousin has only just arrived after her long journey.   She’s delicate, and we’ve only managed to calm her down.

104.source:
快別再提了。」

熙鳳聽了，忙轉悲為喜道：「正是呢。
104.translation:
Now don’t bring it up again.”

 When she heard this, Xifeng instantly switched from grief to happiness.   “How true!” she exclaimed.

105.source: 我一見了妹妹，一心都在他身上，又是歡喜，又是傷心，竟忘了老祖宗了。
105.translation: “The moment I saw my cousin, I became so fixated on her – both with joy and sorrow – that I forgot about Grandmother Jia!

106.source: 該打，該打。」又忙拉著黛玉的手問道：「妹妹幾歲了？
106.translation: Still holding Daiyu’s hand, Xifeng asked her a flurry of concerned questions.   “How old are you, dear cousin?

107.source: 可也上過學？現吃什麼藥？
107.translation: Have you been to school?   What medicines are you taking now?

108.source: 在這裡別想家。要什麼吃的，什麼玩的，只管告訴我。
108.translation: Don’t worry about home here.   If you want something good to eat or any amusements, just tell me.

109.source: 丫頭老婆們不好，也只管告訴我。
109.translation: And if any maids or servants misbehave, make sure you report them to me at once!”

110.source: 」黛玉一一答應。一面熙鳳又問人：「林姑娘的東西可搬進來了？
110.translation: Daiyu answered each of these questions in turn.   Meanwhile, Xifeng turned to the servants and asked, “Has Miss Lin’s luggage been brought in yet?

111.source: 帶了幾個人來？你們趕早打掃兩間下房，讓她們去歇歇。
111.translation: How many maids did she bring?   Hurry up and get two spare rooms ready.

112.source:
」

說話時，已擺了茶果上來。
112.translation: Tea and refreshments were served as they spoke.

113.source: 熙鳳親自佈讓。又見二舅母問他：「月錢放完了沒有？
113.translation: Xifeng personally saw to everybody’s portions.   And then Daiyu’s aunt Lady Wang asked a question: “Have the monthly allowances been distributed yet?”

115.source: 剛才帶了人到後樓上找緞子，找了半日，也沒見昨兒太太說的那個，想必太太記錯了。
115.translation: “Just now I took some maids to search the back upstairs store room for that satin you mentioned.   We combed through it for hours, but couldn’t find the pattern you described yesterday.   Perhaps you misremembered, madam.”

116.translation: “Whether it’s there or not doesn’t really matter,” replied Lady Wang.

117.source: 」因又說道：「該隨手拿出兩個來給你這妹妹裁衣裳啊。
117.translation: She then added, “You should’ve just taken out a few bolts for your cousin’s new clothes.

118.source: 等晚上想著再叫人去拿罷。
118.translation: But it doesn’t matter – just have someone get them tonight.”

120.source: 知道妹妹這兩日必到，我已經預備下了，等太太回去過了目好送來。
120.translation: “Since I knew my cousin would arrive any day, I prepared some silks in advance — pending your approval, of course, Madam.”

121.translation: Lady Wang responded with a faint smile and a silent nod.

122.source: 當下茶果已撤，賈母命兩個老嬤嬤帶黛玉去見兩個舅舅去。
122.translation: After the tea service was finished, Grandmother Jia had two elderly maids escort Daiyu to meet her maternal uncles.

123.source: 賈赦之妻邢氏忙起身笑回道：「我帶了外甥女兒過去，到底便宜些。
123.translation: Jia She’s wife, Lady Xing, rose promptly with a smile.   “Let me take her there,” she said.

125.translation: “Go along with her – no need to return afterwards.”

126.source:
」

那邢夫人答應了，遂帶著黛玉和王夫人作辭。
126.translation: After Lady Xing answered, she took Daiyu’s hand.   They made their farewells to Lady Wang.

127.source: 大家送至穿堂垂花門前。
127.translation: They made their farewells to Lady Wang.   The assembled family then accompanied them to the drooping flower gate.

128.source: 早有眾小廝拉過一輛翠幄清油車來，邢夫人攜了黛玉坐上。
128.translation: A green-canopied carriage, freshly painted and drawn by servants, stood waiting.   Lady Xing guided Daiyu inside as they took their seats.

129.source: 眾婆子放下車簾，方命小廝們抬起，拉至寬處，駕上馴騾，出了西角門，往東過榮府正門，入一黑油漆大門內，至儀門前，方下了車。
129.translation: The older maids lowered the carriage curtains before telling the servants to lift up the carriage.   The servants pulled it to an open area, hitched up the mules, and exited through the western side gate.   Turning east past the main entrance of the Rong Mansion, they entered through a big black gate, going forward until they reached the ceremonial entrance.

130.source: 邢夫人挽著黛玉的手進入院中。
130.translation: Lady Xing led Daiyu into the courtyard by the hand.

131.source: 黛玉度其處必是榮府中之花園隔斷過來的。
131.translation: Daiyu guessed that this section must have been partitioned off from the Rong Mansion’s main gardens.

132.source: 進入三層儀門，果見正房廂房遊廊，悉皆小巧別緻，不似那邊的軒峻壯麗，且院中隨處之樹木山石皆好。
132.translation: And, when they passed through the three ceremonial gates, they indeed did see the principal quarters, side wings, and covered walkways – all of which were exquisitely compact and distinctive, though they lacked the soaring grandeur of the rest of the mansion.   And yet every tree and rock in the courtyard was arranged with perfect grace.

133.source: 及進入正室，早有許多豔妝麗服之姬妾丫鬟迎著。
133.translation: When they entered the main hall, they were greeted by a crowd of concubines and maids in resplendent dresses and radiant makeup.

134.source: 邢夫人讓黛玉坐了，一面令人到外書房中請賈赦。
134.translation: Lady Xing had Daiyu sit while she sent a servant to summon Jia She from his outer study.

135.source: 一時回來說：「老爺說了：『連日身上不好，見了姑娘，彼此傷心，暫且不忍相見。
135.translation: “The master says: ‘Since I’ve been unwell these days, a meeting with the young lady would only grieve us both; I can’t meet her for now.

136.source: 勸姑娘不必傷懷想家，跟著老太太和舅母是和家裡一樣的。
136.translation: Tell her that she shouldn’t think too much of home.   Staying with Grandmother Jia and her aunts is just like being among family.

137.source: 姐妹們雖拙，大家一處作伴，也可以解些煩悶。
137.translation: Though her cousins might be a bit foolish, they’ll at least keep her company.

138.source: 或有委屈之處，只管說，別外道了才是。
138.translation: Though her cousins might be a bit foolish, they’ll at least keep her company.   If she has any trouble, she should let me know and not treat me like a stranger.’”

139.source:
』」

黛玉忙站起身來一一答應了，再坐一刻，便告辭。
139.translation: Daiyu rose promptly and answered each instruction.   After sitting for a while longer, she rose to leave.

140.source: 邢夫人苦留吃過飯才去，黛玉笑回道：「舅母愛惜賜飯，原不應辭，只是還要過去拜見二舅舅，恐去遲了不恭。
140.translation: Lady Xing told her to earnestly to stay for the meal, but Daiyu smiled in reply.   “Dear aunt, I shouldn’t refuse your kind offer.   But I still need to pay my respects to my second uncle, and I worry that delaying would be rude.

141.translation: But I still need to pay my respects to my second uncle, and I worry that delaying would be rude.   Might I beg your indulgence to enjoy your company another day?”

142.translation: “I suppose you’re right,” conceded Lady Xing with a smile.

143.source: 」遂命兩個嬤嬤用方才坐來的車送過去。
143.translation: She then instructed two older maids to escort Daiyu back in the same carriage that had brought them.

144.source: 於是黛玉告辭。邢夫人送至儀門前，又囑咐了眾人幾句，眼看著車去了方回來。
144.translation: Daiyu bid farewell.   Lady Xing took her as far as the ceremonial gate, where she issued her final orders to the attendants.   She only went back inside after watching the carriage depart.

146.source: 一時，黛玉進入榮府，下了車，只見一條大甬路，直接出大門來。
146.translation: Daiyu soon arrived at the Rong Mansion and exited the carriage.   There was a wide paved path in front of her leading straight to the main gate.

147.source: 眾嬤嬤引著，便往東轉彎，走過一座東西穿堂，向南大廳之後，至儀門內大院落。
147.translation: The older maids there guided her through a hallway to the east and past the southern main hall, where they reached a grand courtyard inside the ceremonial gate.

148.source: 上面五間大正房，兩邊廂房，鹿頂耳門鑽山，四通八達，軒昂壯麗，比各處不同。
148.translation: Five imposing main halls stood in front of her, flanked by small rooms on both sides.

149.source: 黛玉便知這方是正內室。
149.translation: Daiyu knew at once that this must be the heart of the mansion.

150.source: 進入堂屋，抬頭迎面先見一個赤金九龍青地大匾，匾上寫著斗大三個字是「榮禧堂」。
150.translation: As she entered the hall, she looked up and saw an enormous plaque of red gold against a blue background.   The plaque was adorned with nine dragons.   Upon the plaque were three bold and large characters proclaiming that this was the “Hall of Glorious Happiness.”

151.source: 後有一行小字：「某年月日書賜榮國公賈源」，又有「萬機宸翰」之寶。
151.translation: Beneath it, in smaller characters, was inscribed: “Bestowed upon Jia Yuan, Duke of Rongguo, on” followed by a date.

152.source: 大紫檀雕螭案上設著三尺多高青綠古銅鼎，懸著待漏隨朝墨龍大畫。
152.translation: There was a large purple sandwood desk that was carved with dragon figures.   On top of it was an ancient bronze tripod, which was about three feet tall and was colored blue and green.

154.source: 地下兩溜十六張楠木圈椅。
154.translation: Along the floor were two rows of armchairs with circular backs, sixteen in all.

155.source: 又有一副對聯，乃是烏木聯牌，鑲著鏨金字跡，道是：「座上珠璣昭日月，堂前黼黻煥煙霞。
155.translation: On the sides of the hall were a pair of plaques made of ebony wood inscribed with golden characters.   They proclaimed:

156.source: 」下面一行小字是：「世教弟勳襲東安郡王穆蒔拜手書。
156.translation: Underneath, a small note identified the calligrapher: “Respectfully inscribed by your younger brother in worldly virtue, Mu Shi, Hereditary Prince of Dong’an Commandery.”

159.source: 原來王夫人時常居坐宴息也不在這正室中，只在東邊的三間耳房內。
159.translation: It turned out that Lady Wang didn’t normally receive guests in the main hall.   Rather, she would receive them in the three small side rooms to the east.

160.source: 於是嬤嬤們引黛玉進東房門來。
160.translation: And so the maids led Daiyu through the eastern door.

161.source: 臨窗大炕上鋪著猩紅洋毯，正面設著大紅金錢蟒引枕，秋香色金錢蟒大條褥。
161.translation: There was a large heated brick bed in this room, with a scarlet foreign rug spread on top of it.   At the head of the bed was a large red pillow embroidered with golden serpent medallions.

162.source: 兩邊設一對梅花式洋漆小几：左邊几上擺著文王鼎，鼎旁匙箸香盒；右邊几上擺著汝窯美人觚，裡面插著時鮮花卉。
162.translation: A pair of painted side tables shaped like plum blossoms were on the sides of the bed.   King Wen’s ritual tripod was on the table on the left, with an incense spoon, tongs, and a fragrant resin box.

163.source: 地下面，西一溜四張大椅都搭著銀紅撒花椅搭，底下四副腳踏；兩邊又有一對高几，几上茗碗瓶花俱備。
163.translation: There were four large armchairs against the western wall, each of which was draped by silver-red floral slipcovers and accompanied by footstools.   On the side of the armchairs were two tall side tables with tea bowls and vases of fresh flowers.

164.translation: As for the rest of the furnishings, we don’t need to detail them any further.

165.translation: An older maid invited Daiyu onto the heated bed.

166.source: 炕沿上卻也有兩個錦褥對設。
166.translation: There were two brocade cushions placed opposite each other along the edge of the bed.

167.source: 黛玉度其位次，便不上炕，只就東邊椅上坐了。
167.translation: Daiyu understood the implied seating hierarchy, and chose not to climb onto the bed.   Instead, she sat on the chair on the eastern side.

168.source: 本房的丫鬟忙捧上茶來。

169.source: 黛玉一面吃茶，一面打量這些丫鬟們，妝飾衣裙，舉止行動，果與別家不同。
169.translation: The maids promptly served tea.   As Daiyu sipped her tea, she noticed the adornments, clothing, and demeanor of the maids, in particular how different they were from other households.

170.source: 茶未吃了，只見一個穿紅綾襖青緞掐牙背心的丫鬟走來笑道：「太太說，請林姑娘到那邊坐罷。
170.translation: Before she could finish her tea, a maid in a red silk jacket and black satin vest trimmed with embroidered borders came and announced with a smile: “Madam requests Miss Lin’s presence in the other room.” When she heard this, the older maid took Daiyu out again, leading her to three small rooms in the southeastern corner.

171.source: 」老嬤嬤聽了，於是又引黛玉出來，到了東南三間小正房內。
171.translation: Before she could finish her tea, a maid in a red silk jacket and black satin vest trimmed with embroidered borders came and announced with a smile: “Madam requests Miss Lin’s presence in the other room.” When she heard this, the older maid took Daiyu out again, leading her to three small rooms in the southeastern corner.

172.source: 正面炕上橫設一張炕桌，上面堆著書籍茶具，靠東壁面西設著半舊的青緞靠背引枕。
172.translation: There was a low table piled up with books and tea utensils across the center of the heated bed in this room.   Against the eastern wall and facing west was a slightly worn large black satin pillow.

173.source: 王夫人卻坐在西邊下首，亦是半舊青緞靠背坐褥，見黛玉來了，便往東讓。
173.translation: Lady Wang sat in the less honorable chair on the western side, which was also furnished with a slightly worn black silk pillow.   When she saw Daiyu arrive, she gestured towards the eastern side.

174.source: 黛玉心中料定這是賈政之位，因見挨炕一溜三張椅子上也搭著半舊的彈墨椅袱，黛玉便向椅上坐了。
174.translation: Sensing that the eastern chair must be Jia Zheng’s seat, Daiyu saw three chairs near the heated bed, each of which was draped with faded ink-splashed chair covers.   She chose to sit there instead.

175.source: 王夫人再三讓他上炕，他方挨王夫人坐下。
175.translation: She chose to sit there instead.   Only after Lady Wang had repeatedly urged her to sit on the heated bed did she finally comply, settling herself beside her aunt.

176.source: 王夫人因說：「你舅舅今日齋戒去了，再見罷。
176.translation: “Your uncle is fasting today,” said Lady Wang.

177.source: 只是有一句話囑咐你：你三個姐妹倒都極好，以後一處唸書，認字，學針線，或偶一頑笑，都有個儘讓的。
177.translation: Just let me give you a little bit of advice.   Your three cousins are all excellent girls.   When you study, practice calligraphy, sew together, or even when you play, they will all yield to you.

178.source: 我就只一件不放心：我有一個孽根禍胎，是家裡的『混世魔王』，今日因往廟裡還願去，尚未回來，晚上你看見就知道了。
178.translation: There’s only one thing I’m worried about.   I’ve got a little sinful scourge, the family’s ‘Demon King of Chaos.’ He’s gone to the temple to fulfill a vow and hasn’t come back yet.   You’ll see for yourself tonight.

179.source: 你只以後不要睬他，你這些姐姐妹妹都不敢沾惹他的。
179.translation: Just don’t pay any attention to him.   None of your cousins dare provoke him.”

180.source:
」

黛玉素聞母親說過，「有個內侄，乃銜玉而生，頑劣異常，不喜讀書，最喜在內幃廝混，外祖母又溺愛，無人敢管。
180.translation: “I have a nephew,” her mother had said, “who was born with jade in his mouth, and yet is unruly beyond measure.   He scorns studying, loiters shamelessly in the women’s quarters, and is spoiled by his grandmother to no end.” After hearing what Lady Wang had said, Daiyu realized that this cousin must have been the one her mother was talking about.

181.source: 」今見王夫人所說，便知是這位表兄，一面陪笑道：「舅母所說，可是那位銜玉而生的哥哥？
181.translation:
He scorns studying, loiters shamelessly in the women’s quarters, and is spoiled by his grandmother to no end.” After hearing what Lady Wang had said, Daiyu realized that this cousin must have been the one her mother was talking about.

 “Are you referring to the older brother born with the jade in his mouth?” asked Daiyu with a courteous smile.

182.source: 在家時記得母親常說，這位哥哥比我大一歲，小名就叫寶玉，性雖憨頑，說待姊妹們卻是極好的。
182.translation: “Back at home I often heard my mother say he was a year older than me, and that they call him Baoyu.   She said that he treats his sisters with exceptional kindness, even though he’s a bit mischievous.

183.source: 況我來了，自然和姊妹們一處，弟兄們是另院別房，豈有沾惹之理？
183.translation: Of course, since I’ve come here, I’ll naturally just stick with my female cousins.   The boys are in separate courtyards: how could there be any chance for interacting?”

184.translation: “You don’t understand,” replied Lady Wang, smiling.

185.source: 他和別人不同，自幼因老太太疼愛，原系和姐妹們一處嬌養慣了的。
185.translation: “He’s not like anybody else.   Grandmother Jia has doted on him since he was a child, and he was raised indulgently among his sisters.

186.source: 若姐妹們不理他，他倒還安靜些；若一日姐妹們和他多說了一句話，他心上一喜，便生出許多事來：所以囑咐你別理會他。
186.translation: If they ignore him, he’ll stay quiet; however, if they even speak a word to him, his heart soars, and then all sorts of trouble starts.   That’s why I’m warning you: don’t indulge him.

187.source: 他嘴裡一時甜言蜜語，一時有天沒日，瘋瘋傻傻，只休信他。
187.translation: One moment he says all sorts of sweet things; the next he’s speaking nonsense and defying logic.   Just ignore him.”

189.source: 忽見一個丫鬟來說：「老太太那裡傳晚飯了。
189.translation: Suddenly, a maid appeared, announcing: “Grandmother Jia has called everyone for dinner.”

190.source: 」王夫人忙攜了黛玉出後房門，由後廊往西出了角門，是一條南北甬路，南邊是倒座三間小小抱廈廳，北邊立著一個粉油大影壁，後有一個半大門，小小一所房屋。
190.translation: Lady Wang hastily took Daiyu by the hand, led her out the back door, and turned west through a covered corridor.   They passed through a side gate and onto a paved walkway that stretched from north to south.   There was a three room miniature reception hall to the south, its back nestled against the main house, and there was a white screen wall to the north.

191.source: 王夫人笑指向黛玉道：「這是你鳳姐姐的屋子，回來你好往這裡找他去。
191.translation: Lady Wang smiled and gestured towards the compound.   “This is where your cousin Xifeng lives,” she told Daiyu.

192.source: 少什麼東西，只管和他說就是了。
192.translation: If you need anything at all, just ask her.”

193.source: 」這院門上也有幾個才總角的小廝，都垂手侍立。
193.translation: At the gate of the courtyard, a few young servant boys, their hair still in childish topknots, stood waiting with their hands respectfully at their sides.

195.source: 王夫人遂攜黛玉穿過一個東西穿堂，便是賈母的後院了，於是進入後房門。
195.translation: Lady Wang led Daiyu through a hallway that ran from east to west.   This brought them to the rear courtyard of Grandmother Jia’s residence.

196.source: 已有許多人在此伺候，見王夫人來，方安設桌椅。
196.translation: They entered through the back door, where many servants were already waiting.   Once they saw Lady Wang arrive, they promptly set up tables and chairs.

197.source: 賈珠之妻李氏捧杯，熙鳳安箸，王夫人進羹。
197.translation: Jia Zhu’s wife Lady Li held the wine cup, Xifeng arranged the chopsticks, and Lady Wang presented the soup.

198.source: 賈母正面榻上獨坐，兩旁四張空椅。
198.translation: Grandmother Jia sat alone on the main couch with two empty chairs arranged on each side.

199.source: 熙鳳忙拉黛玉在左邊第一張椅子上坐下，黛玉十分推讓。
199.translation: Xifeng promptly pulled Daiyu to sit in the first chair on the left, but Daiyu politely declined.

200.source: 賈母笑道：「你舅母和嫂子們是不在這裡吃飯的，你是客，原該這麼坐。
200.translation: “Your aunt and sisters-in-law won’t be dining here,” smiled Grandmother Jia in response.   “You’re the guest.

201.translation:
Of course you should sit here.”

 Daiyu then politely asked permission before sitting.

202.translation: Grandmother Jia instructed Lady Wang to be seated as well.

203.source: 迎春姊妹三個告了坐方上來，迎春坐右手第一，探春左第二，惜春右第二。
203.translation: Yingchun took the first seat on the right, Tanchun the second on the left, and Xichun the second on the right.

204.source: 旁邊丫鬟執著拂塵漱盂巾帕。
204.translation: Numerous maids stood at hand with whisks, rinsing bowls, and towels.

205.source: 李紈鳳姐立於案旁佈讓。
205.translation: Li Wan and Xifeng remained standing by the table to supervise.

206.source: 外間伺候的媳婦丫鬟雖多，卻連一聲咳嗽不聞。
206.translation: Though there were many serving women and maids waiting in the outer chambers, they were absolutely silent, not even coughing.

207.source: 飯畢，各各有丫鬟用小茶盤捧上茶來。
207.translation: When the meal concluded, the maids presented tea to each person on small trays.

208.source: 當日林家教女以惜福養身，每飯後必過片時方吃茶，不傷脾胃。
208.translation:
When the meal concluded, the maids presented tea to each person on small trays.

 The Lin family had raised its daughter to cherish health and nurture her constitution.

209.source: 今黛玉見了這裡許多規矩不似家中，也只得隨和著些。
209.translation: However, seeing that many of the customs here differed from her family, Daiyu felt obliged to adapt.

210.source: 接了茶，又有人捧過漱盂來，黛玉也漱了口。
210.translation: She accepted the tea, but a maid then presented a rinsing bowl, and so she rinsed her mouth instead.

211.source: 又盥手畢，然後又捧上茶來，這方是吃的茶。
211.translation: The maids only offered tea again after she finished washing her hands; this time she drank it properly.

213.source: 賈母便說：「你們去罷，讓我們自在說說話兒。
213.translation: “You may leave now,” said Grandmother Jia.   “Let’s stay and chat a little.”

214.source: 」王夫人遂起身，又說了兩句閒話兒，方引李鳳二人去了。
214.translation: Lady Wang rose to her feet.   After a few more casual remarks, she left, taking Li Wan and Xifeng with her.

215.source: 賈母因問黛玉念何書，黛玉道：「剛唸了《四書》。
215.translation:
Grandmother Jia asked Daiyu what books she had been studying.

 “I’ve just finished reading The Four Books,” replied Daiyu.

216.source: 」黛玉又問姊妹們讀何書，賈母道：「讀什麼書！
216.translation:
Daiyu then asked what books the other girls were reading.

 “Books?” replied Grandmother Jia.

217.translation:
“They’ve only learned a few characters – that’s all.”

 Before she could finish speaking, however, there was a flurry of footsteps outside.

218.source: 一語未了，只聽外面一陣腳步響，丫鬟進來報道寶玉來了。
218.translation: Before she could finish speaking, however, there was a flurry of footsteps outside.   A maid rushed in, announcing that Baoyu had just arrived.

219.source: 黛玉心想：「這個寶玉不知是怎樣個憊懶人呢。
219.translation: “I wonder what kind of unruly scamp this Baoyu is,” thought Daiyu to herself.

220.source: 」及至進來一看，卻是位青年公子。
220.translation: And yet, when he entered, she saw a young, attractive gentleman.

221.source: 頭上戴著束髮嵌寶紫金冠，齊眉勒著二龍戲珠金抹額；一件二色金百蝶穿花大紅箭袖，束著五彩絲攢花結長穗宮絛，外罩石青起花八團倭緞排穗褂；登著青緞粉底小朝靴。
221.translation: He had a purple and gold crown upon his head that was inlaid with jewels.   The crown was fastened at the brow with a golden headband, which was embroidered with a motif of two dragons playing with a pearl.   His robe, a vibrant crimson archer’s sleeve adorned with butterflies and flowers in two colored golden thread, was cinched at the waist by a long, tasseled multicolored silk sash which was tied into floral knots.

222.source: 面若中秋之月，色如春曉之花，鬢若刀裁，眉如墨畫，鼻如懸膽，睛若秋波。
222.translation: His hairline was as sharp as if it were cut by a blade, his eyebrows were as dark as ink paintings.   His nose has a noble arch to it, and his eyes shimmered like autumn waves.

223.source: 雖怒時而似笑，即瞋視而有情。
223.translation: Even when he was anger, he seemed to smile.

224.source: 項上金螭纓絡，又有一根五色絲絛，繫著一塊美玉。
224.translation: There was a golden chained pendant of coiled dragons around his neck, as well as a five colored silk cord, from which hung a beautiful piece of jade.

225.source: 黛玉一見便吃一大驚，心中想道：「好生奇怪！
225.translation: Daiyu was shocked as she first saw him.   “How extraordinary!” she thought to herself.

226.source: 倒像在那裡見過的？何等眼熟！
226.translation: Why does he look so familiar?”

227.source: 」只見這寶玉向賈母請了安，賈母便命：「去見你娘來。
227.translation: Meanwhile, Baoyu greeted Grandmother Jia, who said to him, “go and greet your mother.”

228.source: 」即轉身去了。一回再來時，已換了冠帶。
228.translation: When Baoyu returned shortly afterwards, he had changed into informal clothes.

229.source: 頭上周圍一轉的短髮，都結成小辮，紅絲結束，共攢至頂中胎髮，總編一根大辮，黑亮如漆，從頂至梢，一串四顆大珠，用金八寶墜腳。
229.translation: The short hair around his head was now braided into tiny plaits tied with red silk threads, all of which were gathered into the longer hair at the top of his head and woven into one thick, glossy, deep black braid.   The braid was adorned from root to tip with four large pearls and fastened at the end with a golden ornament of the eight treasures.

230.source: 身上穿著銀紅撒花半舊大襖，仍舊戴著項圈、寶玉、寄名鎖、護身符等物；下面半露鬆綠撒花綾褲，錦邊彈墨襪，厚底大紅鞋。
230.translation: He wore a well-worn silver and red floral jacket, which was still adorned with his golden necklet, precious jade, protective amulets, and the longevity lock from his childhood naming ceremony.   Below this were loose emerald-green silk trousers with scattered blossoms, paired with ink-splashed stockings trimmed in brocade and thick-soled scarlet shoes.

231.source: 越顯得面如敷粉，脣若施脂，轉盼多情，語言若笑。
231.translation: His powdered face seemed even more radiant, and his lips looked as if they were colored with carmine.   Every glance sparkled with tender feeling, and every word seemed to smile.

232.source: 天然一段風韻，全在眉梢；平生萬種情思，悉堆眼角。
232.translation: There was a natural charm in the arch of his eyebrows, while a lifetime of romantic longing gathered at the corners of his eyes.

233.source: 看其外貌是極好，卻難知其底細。
233.translation: At first glance his appearance was exceptional.   And yet his true nature remained elusive.

234.source: 後人有《西江月》二詞，批的極確。
234.translation: Later commentators captured this perfectly in two poems from The Moon on the West River.

235.source:
詞曰：

無故尋愁覓恨，有時似傻如狂。
235.translation:
The first poem read:

 Without cause, he courts sorrow and hunts grief,

236.source: 縱然生得好皮囊，腹內原來草莽。
236.translation:
That fine skin wrapped around him,

 A belly stuffed with reckless weeds.

237.source: 潦倒不通世務，愚頑怕讀文章。
237.translation: Dull and stubborn, he flees from books.

238.source: 行為偏僻性乖張，那管世人誹謗？
238.translation:
Eccentric in deed, perverse in temper,

 Who cares if the whole world scolds?

239.source:
又曰：

富貴不知樂業，貧窮難耐淒涼。
239.translation:
In wealth, he knows no joy in work;

 In poverty, he would bear no hardship.

240.source: 可憐辜負好時光，於國於家無望。
240.translation:
Wretched, he wastes his precious youth,

 Useless to state and household.

241.source: 天下無能第一，古今不肖無雙。
241.translation:
Number one in uselessness under heaven,

 Historically unmatched in unfilialness.

242.source: 寄言紈袴與膏粱：莫效此兒形狀！
242.translation:
A message to wealthy people everywhere:

 Never copy this child’s manner!

244.source: 卻說賈母見他進來，笑道：「外客沒見就脫了衣裳了？
244.translation: Grandmother Jia saw him enter.   “I see you changed clothes before greeting our guest,” she said with a smile.

245.source: 還不去見你妹妹呢。」寶玉早已看見了一個嫋嫋婷婷的女兒，便料定是林姑媽之女，忙來見禮。
245.translation: Baoyu had already seen the slender, graceful young girl, and figured that she must be the daughter of his Aunt Lin.   He stepped forward to greet her.

246.source: 歸了座，細看時，真是與眾各別。
246.translation: Once he was seated, he took a closer look at her.   He saw that she was really unlike anyone he had ever seen.

247.source:
只見：

兩彎似蹙非蹙籠煙眉，一雙似喜非喜含情目。
247.translation:
Her eyebrows were like a faint mist, neither knit nor smooth.

 Her eyes had a dreamy sweetness, neither glad nor sad.

248.source: 態生兩靨之愁，嬌襲一身之病。
248.translation: Her fragile frame seemed to be wrapped in illness.

249.translation:
There were glistening teardrops on her eyelashes.

 Soft sighs faintly escaped her lips.

250.source: 閒靜似嬌花照水，行動如弱柳扶風。
250.translation:
In stillness, she looked like a flower mirrored in water;

 In motion, she was like a willow swaying in the wind.

251.source: 心較比干多一竅，病如西子勝三分。
251.translation:
She was more perceptive than Bi Gan,

 And she was three times as sickly as Xi Shi.

253.source: 寶玉看罷，笑道：「這個妹妹，我曾見過的。
253.translation: “I’ve seen this cousin before,” said Baoyu, looking at Lin Daiyu and smiling.

254.translation: “You’re talking nonsense again,” said Grandmother Jia with a laugh.

255.source: 你何曾見過？」寶玉笑道：「雖沒見過，卻看著面善，心裡倒像是舊相認識，恍若遠別重逢的一般。
255.translation:
“How could you have seen her?”

 “Well, even if I haven’t, she looks familiar,” replied Baoyu.   “It’s like meeting an old friend after a long separation.

258.source: 寶玉便走向黛玉身邊坐下，又細細打量一番，因問：「妹妹可曾讀書？
258.translation: Baoyu sat next to Daiyu, studying her closely once more.   “Have you studied much?” he asked.

259.source: 」黛玉道：「不曾讀書，只上了一年學，些須認得幾個字。
259.translation: “Not much,” replied Daiyu.   “I attended school for just a year, and only know a few characters.”

260.translation: “And what is your given name?” asked Baoyu.

261.source: 」黛玉便說了名。寶玉又道：「表字？
261.translation:
“And what is your given name?” asked Baoyu.

 Daiyu told him.

262.source: 」黛玉道：「無字。」寶玉笑道：「我送妹妹一字，莫若『顰顰』二字，極妙。
262.translation:
“No,” she said.

 “Then let me give you one,” smiled Baoyu.

263.translation: “And what is the source of this name?” asked Tanchun.

264.source: 」寶玉道：「《古今人物通考》上說：『西方有石名黛，可代畫眉之墨。
264.translation: “The Comprehensive Study of People, Ancient and Modern states: ‘In the West, there is a stone called dai which may be ground into ink for painting eyebrows.’ Besides, this cousin’s eyebrows are delicately arched.

265.source: 』況這妹妹，眉尖若蹙，取這個字，豈不甚美？

266.translation: “I think you’re just making this up!” laughed Tanchun.

267.source: 」寶玉笑道：「除了《四書》，杜撰的也太多呢。
267.translation: “Well, most things are made up,” replied Baoyu.   “Except for the Four Books, that is.”

268.translation: “Do you have a jade?” he asked.

269.source: 」眾人都不解。黛玉便忖度著「因他有玉，所以才問我的」，便答道：「我沒有玉。
269.translation:
“Do you have a jade?” he asked.

 Nobody else understood.

 “He must be asking me this because he has a jade,” thought Daiyu to herself.

270.source: 你那玉也是件稀罕物兒，豈能人人皆有？
270.translation: “The jade that you have is extremely rare.   How could everybody have one?”

271.source:
」

寶玉聽了，登時發作起狂病來，摘下那玉，就狠命摔去，罵道：「什麼罕物！
271.translation: When he heard this, Baoyu threw a fit.   Snatching off the jade, he threw it down with all his might.   “What rare treasure?” he screamed.

272.source: 人的高下不識，還說靈不靈呢！

273.source: 我也不要這勞什子！」嚇的地下眾人一擁爭去拾玉。
273.translation:
I don’t want to have anything to do with this wretched thing!”

 Everybody was so shocked by this that they all hurried over to pick up the jade.

274.source: 賈母急的摟了寶玉，道：「孽障！
274.translation: Grandmother Jia hurried to hug Baoyu.   “You little demon!” she cried.

275.source: 你生氣，要打罵人容易，何苦摔那命根子！
275.translation: “You little demon!” she cried.   “If you’re angry, you can scream at or hit people.

276.source: 」寶玉滿面淚痕，哭道：「家裡姐姐妹妹都沒有，單我有，我說沒趣兒；如今來了這個神仙似的妹妹也沒有，可知這不是個好東西。
276.translation: With tears streaming down his face, Baoyu sobbed.   “None of my sisters and cousins have one; only I do.   What’s the point of that?   And now this fairy-like cousin doesn’t have one, either.

277.source: 」賈母忙哄他道：「你這妹妹原有玉來著，因你姑媽去世時，捨不得你妹妹，無法可處，遂將他的玉帶了去：一則全殉葬之禮，盡你妹妹的孝心；二則你姑媽的陰靈兒也可權作見了你妹妹了。
277.translation: “Actually, this cousin of yours originally did have jade,” said Grandmother Jia, soothing him.   “But when your aunt passed away, she couldn’t bear to be separated from her daughter.   Since there was nothing else she could do, she took her daughter’s jade with her to the grave.   First, this fulfilled the burial rites and showed the level of your cousin’s filial devotion.

278.source: 因此，他說沒有，也是不便誇張的意思啊。
278.translation: That’s why she says she has none now.   There’s also no point in her boasting about it.

279.source: 你還不好生帶上，仔細你娘知道！
279.translation: Now put yours back on at once, or your mother will hear about this!” And with that she took the jade from the maid’s hands and fastened it around his neck herself.

280.source: 」說著，便向丫鬟手中接來，親與他帶上。
280.translation: Now put yours back on at once, or your mother will hear about this!” And with that she took the jade from the maid’s hands and fastened it around his neck herself.

281.source: 寶玉聽如此說，想了一想，也就不生別論。
281.translation: When Baoyu heard what she said, he paused, thought for a little while, and let the matter drop.

283.source: 當下奶孃來問黛玉房舍。
283.translation: A senior maid came in just then to ask about where Daiyu would be staying.

284.source: 賈母便說：「將寶玉挪出來，同我在套間暖閣裡，把林姑娘暫且安置在碧紗櫥裡。
284.translation: “Have Baoyu stay with me in my room,” said Grandmother Jia.   “Have Miss Lin stay in the room with the green gauze screen.

285.source: 等過了殘冬，春天再給他們收拾房屋，另作一番安置罷。
285.translation: Once winter is over and spring comes, we’ll properly arrange their rooms and adjust the accommodations.”

287.source: 我就在碧紗櫥外的床上很妥當，又何必出來鬧的老祖宗不得安靜呢？
287.translation: “I’d be perfectly fine on the bed just outside the green gauze room.   Why should I have to move out and disturb your peace?”

288.translation: After thinking for a moment, Grandmother Jia agreed.   “That would be fine.”

289.source: 」每人一個奶孃並一個丫頭照管，餘者在外間上夜聽喚。
289.translation: Each of them was assigned an older maid and a younger maid for personal care.   The rest of the attendants stayed in the outer room in case they were needed.

290.source: 一面早有熙鳳命人送了一頂藕合色花帳並錦被緞褥之類。
290.translation: In the meantime, Xifeng had already had somebody bring over a pale purple flower canopy, along with brocade quilts and satin bedding.

291.source: 黛玉只帶了兩個人來：一個是自己的奶孃王嬤嬤，一個是十歲的小丫頭，名喚雪雁。
291.translation: Daiyu had only brought two servants with her.   One was an older made, Nanny Wang, and the other was a ten-year-old maid named Xueyan.

292.source: 賈母見雪雁甚小，一團孩氣，王嬤嬤又極老，料黛玉皆不遂心，將自己身邊一個二等小丫頭，名喚鸚哥的，與了黛玉。
292.translation: Grandmother Jia noticed that Xueyan was just a child, and that Nanny Wang was quite old, and thought that neither would suit Daiyu.   And so she gave Daiyu one of her own second rank maids, a young girl named Yingge.

293.source: 亦如迎春等一般：每人除自幼乳母外，另有四個教引嬤嬤；除貼身掌管釵釧盥沐兩個丫頭外，另有四五個灑掃房屋來往使役的小丫頭。
293.translation: In addition to an older maid, each had four older maids as teachers.   And, in addition to two personal maids responsible for hair ornaments, jewelry, and bathing, there were another four or five junior maids assigned to cleaning, sweeping, and running errands.

294.source: 當下王嬤嬤與鸚哥陪侍黛玉在碧紗櫥內；寶玉乳母李嬤嬤並大丫頭名喚襲人的陪侍在外面大床上。
294.translation: After that, Nanny Wang and Yingge went inside the green gauze room.   Meanwhile, Baoyu’s older maid, Nanny Li, along with his chief maid Xiren, looked after him on the large bed outside the room.

295.source: 原來這襲人亦是賈母之婢，本名蕊珠。
295.translation: Xiren had originally been one of Grandmother Jia’s maids.   Her given name was Ruizhu.

296.source: 賈母因溺愛寶玉，恐寶玉之婢不中使，素喜蕊珠心地純良，遂與寶玉。
296.translation: However, Grandmother Jia doted on Baoyu, and worried that his maids might not suit him.   She had admired Ruizhu’s gentle nature for a long time, and so she assigned her to be Baoyu’s maid.

297.source: 寶玉因知他本姓花，又曾見舊人詩句有「花氣襲人」之句，遂回明賈母，即把蕊珠更名襲人。
297.translation: Baoyu knew that her surname was Hua, and remembered an ancient poem line that read “the fragrance of flowers assails men,” and so he successfully convinced Grandmother Jia to change her name to Xiren.

298.source: 卻說這襲人倒有些痴處：伏侍賈母時，心中只有賈母；如今跟了寶玉，心中又只有寶玉了。
298.translation: However, there was something unusual about Xiren.   When she served Grandmother Jia, she was completely loyal to Grandmother Jia.   Now that she served Baoyu, her whole heart turned solely to Baoyu.

299.source: 只因寶玉性情乖僻，每每規諫，見寶玉不聽，心中著實憂鬱。
299.translation: And yet Baoyu’s eccentric temperament often caused her to admonish him.   When he would refuse to listen to her, she’d feel depressed.

300.source: 是晚，寶玉李嬤嬤已睡了。
300.translation: That evening, after Baoyu and Nanny Li had fallen asleep, Xiren noticed that Daiyu and Yingge were still awake.

301.source: 他見裡面黛玉鸚哥猶未安歇，他自卸了妝，悄悄的進來，笑問：「姑娘怎麼還不安歇？
301.translation: Xiren had already gotten ready for bed, and so she slipped inside quietly, asking Daiyu “why haven’t you gone to sleep yet?”

302.translation: “Please have a seat, sister,” replied Daiyu.

303.translation: Xiren sat down on the edge of the bed.

304.source: 鸚哥笑道：「林姑娘在這裡傷心，自己淌眼抹淚的說：『今兒才來了，就惹出你家哥兒的狂病來。
304.translation: “Miss Lin was grieving earlier, wiping away tears and saying ‘I’ve barely arrived, and yet I’ve already caused him to throw a fit.

305.source: 倘或摔壞了那玉，豈不是因我之過？
305.translation: If he had broken that jade, wouldn’t it have all been my fault?’ That’s why she was so upset.

306.translation: I only just managed to calm her down.”

307.translation: “Don’t take it so hard, miss!” responded Xiren.

308.source: 將來只怕比這更奇怪的笑話兒還有呢。
308.translation: “I worry that you’ll encounter even stranger things.

309.source: 若為他這種行狀，你多心傷感，只怕你還傷感不了呢。
309.translation: If you worry this much over every one of his outbursts, you’ll never have peace.

310.source: 快別多心！」黛玉道：「姐姐們說的，我記著就是了。
310.translation:
Don’t worry so much!”

 “I’ll remember what you sisters told me,” said Daiyu.

311.translation: They spoke a while longer before going to sleep.

312.source: 次早起來，省過賈母，因往王夫人處來。
312.translation: The next morning, after she paid respects to Grandmother Jia, Daiyu went to Lady Wang’s room.

313.source: 正值王夫人與熙鳳在一處拆金陵來的書信，又有王夫人的兄嫂處遣來的兩個媳婦兒來說話。
313.translation: She arrived just as Lady Wang and Xifeng were reading letters from Jinling.   There were two older maids sent by Lady Wang’s elder brother and sister-in-law who were also there to discuss matters.

314.source: 黛玉雖不知原委，探春等卻曉得是議論金陵城中居住的薛家姨母之子，表兄薛蟠，倚財仗勢打死人命，現在應天府案下審理。
314.translation: Though Daiyu didn’t know the details, Tanchun and the others knew they were talking about the son of Aunt Xue from Jinling.   This was their cousin Xue Pan, who had allegedly used his wealth and influence to commit murder.   His case was under review by the Yingtian Prefectural Court.

315.source: 如今舅舅王子騰得了信，遣人來告訴這邊，意欲喚取進京之意。
315.translation: After he heard about this case, their uncle Wang Ziteng had sent messengers with a letter to warn the family.   It seems that he wanted to send the Xue family to the capitol.

316.translation: And, if you want to know what happens next, read the next chapter.
